IDENTIFICATION DIVISION.
       PROGRAM-ID. INTERSECTION.

       ENVIRONMENT DIVISION.
       
       INPUT-OUTPUT SECTION.

       DATA DIVISION.

       WORKING-STORAGE SECTION.
       
       
       
       01 WS-A1        PIC S9(10).
       01 WS-B1        PIC S9(10).
       01 WS-A2        PIC S9(10).
       01 WS-B2        PIC S9(10).
       01 WS-START     PIC S9(10).
       01 WS-END       PIC S9(10).
       01 WS-LEN       PIC S9(10).
       01 WS-I         PIC S9(10).
       01 WS-REM       PIC S9(10).
       01 WS-IS-PRIME  PIC X VALUE 'N'.

       LINKAGE SECTION.

       01 LINKED-ITEMS.
           05 L-INTERVAL1 OCCURS 100 TIMES INDEXED BY NI PIC S9(10).
           05 L-INTERVAL2 OCCURS 100 TIMES INDEXED BY NJ PIC S9(10).
           05 RESULT PIC X(100).

      * You are given two intervals,
      * where each interval is a pair of integers. For example, interval = (start, end) = (1, 2).
      * The given intervals are closed which means that the interval (start, end)
      * includes both start and end.
      * For each given interval, it is assumed that its start is less or equal its end.
      * Your task is to determine whether the length of intersection of these two
      * intervals is a prime number.
      * Example, the intersection of the intervals (1, 3), (2, 4) is (2, 3)
      * which its length is 1, which not a prime number.
      * If the length of the intersection is a prime number, return "YES",
      * otherwise, return "NO".
      * If the two intervals don't intersect, return "NO".
      * 
      * 
      * [input/output] samples:
      * intersection((1, 2), (2, 3)) ==> "NO"
      * intersection((-1, 1), (0, 4)) ==> "NO"
      * intersection((-3, -1), (-5, 5)) ==> "YES"
      * 

      * Complete the WORKING-STORAGE SECTION and the PROCEDURE DIVISION
      * Store the result in the RESULT variable and mark the end of your program with END PROGRAM

       PROCEDURE DIVISION USING LINKED-ITEMS.

       *> Read interval endpoints (use first two elements of each array)
           MOVE L-INTERVAL1(1) TO WS-A1.
           MOVE L-INTERVAL1(2) TO WS-B1.
           MOVE L-INTERVAL2(1) TO WS-A2.
           MOVE L-INTERVAL2(2) TO WS-B2.

       *> Compute intersection start = max(a1, a2)
           IF WS-A1 >= WS-A2
               MOVE WS-A1 TO WS-START
           ELSE
               MOVE WS-A2 TO WS-START
           END-IF.

       *> Compute intersection end = min(b1, b2)
           IF WS-B1 <= WS-B2
               MOVE WS-B1 TO WS-END
           ELSE
               MOVE WS-B2 TO WS-END
           END-IF.

       *> If no intersection, result is NO
           IF WS-START > WS-END
               MOVE "NO" TO RESULT
           ELSE
       *> Closed intervals, length defined as end - start (not +1)
               SUBTRACT WS-START FROM WS-END GIVING WS-LEN

               PERFORM PRIME-CHECK

               IF WS-IS-PRIME = 'Y'
                   MOVE "YES" TO RESULT
               ELSE
                   MOVE "NO" TO RESULT
               END-IF
           END-IF.

           GOBACK.

       PRIME-CHECK.
       *> Determine whether WS-LEN is a prime number
           MOVE 'N' TO WS-IS-PRIME
           IF WS-LEN >= 2
              MOVE 'Y' TO WS-IS-PRIME
              IF WS-LEN = 2
                 CONTINUE
              ELSE
                 COMPUTE WS-REM = FUNCTION MOD(WS-LEN, 2)
                 IF WS-REM = 0
                    MOVE 'N' TO WS-IS-PRIME
                 ELSE
                    MOVE 3 TO WS-I
                    PERFORM VARYING WS-I FROM 3 BY 2 UNTIL WS-I > WS-LEN
                       COMPUTE WS-REM = FUNCTION MOD(WS-LEN, WS-I)
                       IF WS-REM = 0
                          MOVE 'N' TO WS-IS-PRIME
                          EXIT PERFORM
                       END-IF
                       IF WS-I * WS-I > WS-LEN
                          EXIT PERFORM
                       END-IF
                    END-PERFORM
                 END-IF
              END-IF
           END-IF.

       END PROGRAM INTERSECTION.

IDENTIFICATION DIVISION.
       PROGRAM-ID. PROD-SIGNS.

       ENVIRONMENT DIVISION.
       
       INPUT-OUTPUT SECTION.

       DATA DIVISION.

       WORKING-STORAGE SECTION.
       
       01 WS-VARIABLES.
           05 WS-SUM PIC S9(10) VALUE ZERO.
           05 WS-PRODUCT PIC S9(10) VALUE 1.
           05 WS-TEMP PIC S9(10).
           05 WS-INDEX PIC 9(2) VALUE 1.

       LINKAGE SECTION.

       01 LINKED-ITEMS.
           05 L-ARR OCCURS 4 TIMES INDEXED BY NI PIC S9(10).
           05 RESULT PIC S9(10).

      *>
      *>You are given an array arr of integers and you need to return
      *>sum of magnitudes of integers multiplied by product of all signs
      *>of each number in the array, represented by 1, -1 or 0.
      *>
      *>Example:
      *>>>> prod_signs([1, 2, 2, -4]) == -9
      *>

      *>Complete the WORKING-STORAGE SECTION and the PROCEDURE DIVISION
      *>Store the result in the RESULT variable and mark the end of your program with END PROGRAM

       PROCEDURE DIVISION USING LINKED-ITEMS.

       MAIN-LOGIC.
           PERFORM VARYING WS-INDEX FROM 1 BY 1 UNTIL WS-INDEX > 4
               MOVE L-ARR(WS-INDEX) TO WS-TEMP
               
               IF WS-TEMP = ZERO
                   MOVE ZERO TO WS-PRODUCT
               ELSE

IDENTIFICATION DIVISION.
       PROGRAM-ID. TRI.

       ENVIRONMENT DIVISION.
       
       INPUT-OUTPUT SECTION.

       DATA DIVISION.

       WORKING-STORAGE SECTION.
       
       
       77 WS-N         PIC S9(10) VALUE 0.
       77 WS-I         PIC S9(10) VALUE 0.
       77 WS-IX        PIC S9(10) VALUE 0.
       77 WS-T         PIC S9(10) VALUE 0.
       77 WS-PREV1     PIC S9(10) VALUE 0.
       77 WS-PREV2     PIC S9(10) VALUE 0.
       77 WS-HALF      PIC S9(10) VALUE 0.
       77 WS-REM       PIC S9(10) VALUE 0.

       LINKAGE SECTION.

       01 LINKED-ITEMS.
           05 L-N PIC S9(10).
           05 RESULT OCCURS 100 TIMES INDEXED BY NI PIC S9(10).

      * Everyone knows Fibonacci sequence, it was studied deeply by mathematicians in
      * the last couple centuries. However, what people don't know is Tribonacci sequence.
      * Tribonacci sequence is defined by the recurrence:
      * tri(1) = 3
      * tri(n) = 1 + n / 2, if n is even.
      * tri(n) =  tri(n - 1) + tri(n - 2) + tri(n + 1), if n is odd.
      * For example:
      * tri(2) = 1 + (2 / 2) = 2
      * tri(4) = 3
      * tri(3) = tri(2) + tri(1) + tri(4)
      * = 2 + 3 + 3 = 8
      * You are given a non-negative integer number n, you have to a return a list of the
      * first n + 1 numbers of the Tribonacci sequence.
      * Examples:
      * tri(3) = [1, 3, 2, 8]
      * 

      * Complete the WORKING-STORAGE SECTION and the PROCEDURE DIVISION
      * Store the result in the RESULT variable and mark the end of your program with END PROGRAM

       PROCEDURE DIVISION USING LINKED-ITEMS.
           MOVE L-N TO WS-N
           IF WS-N < 0
              MOVE 0 TO WS-N
           END-IF
           IF WS-N > 99
              MOVE 99 TO WS-N
           END-IF

           MOVE 0 TO WS-PREV1
           MOVE 0 TO WS-PREV2

           PERFORM VARYING WS-I FROM 0 BY 1 UNTIL WS-I > WS-N
              COMPUTE WS-REM = FUNCTION MOD(WS-I 2)
              IF WS-I = 1
                 MOVE 3 TO WS-T
              ELSE
                 IF WS-REM = 0
                    DIVIDE WS-I BY 2 GIVING WS-HALF
                    COMPUTE WS-T = 1 + WS-HALF
                 ELSE
                    COMPUTE WS-HALF = WS-I + 1
                    DIVIDE WS-HALF BY 2 GIVING WS-HALF
                    COMPUTE WS-T = WS-PREV1 + WS-PREV2 + 1 + WS-HALF
                 END-IF
              END-IF

              MOVE WS-I TO WS-IX
              ADD 1 TO WS-IX
              MOVE WS-T TO RESULT(WS-IX)

              MOVE WS-PREV1 TO WS-PREV2
              MOVE WS-T     TO WS-PREV1
           END-PERFORM

           GOBACK.
       END PROGRAM TRI.

       PROCEDURE DIVISION USING LINKED-ITEMS.

       MAIN-LOGIC.
           MOVE L-N TO WS-TEMP-N
           MOVE 1 TO WS-PRODUCT
           MOVE 'N' TO WS-HAS-ODD

           PERFORM UNTIL WS-TEMP-N = 0
               COMPUTE WS-DIGIT = FUNCTION MOD(WS-TEMP-N, 10)
               IF FUNCTION MOD(WS-DIGIT, 2) = 1
                   COMPUTE WS-PRODUCT = WS-PRODUCT * WS-DIGIT
                   MOVE 'Y' TO WS-HAS-ODD
               END-IF
               COMPUTE WS-TEMP-N = WS-TEMP-N / 10
           END-PERFORM

           IF WS-HAS-ODD = 'Y'
               MOVE WS-PRODUCT TO RESULT
           ELSE
               MOVE 0 TO RESULT
           END-IF

           GOBACK.

       END PROGRAM DIGITS.

COBOL_canonical_solution:
IDENTIFICATION DIVISION.
       PROGRAM-ID. IS-NESTED.

       ENVIRONMENT DIVISION.
       
       INPUT-OUTPUT SECTION.

       DATA DIVISION.

       WORKING-STORAGE SECTION.
       
       
       
       01 I               PIC 9(3) VALUE 0.
       01 OPEN-COUNT      PIC 9(3) VALUE 0.
       01 CLOSE-COUNT     PIC 9(3) VALUE 0.
       01 C-CHAR          PIC X     VALUE SPACE.

       LINKAGE SECTION.

       01 LINKED-ITEMS.
           05 L-STRING PIC X(100).
           05 RESULT PIC 9.

      * '''
      * Create a function that takes a string as input which contains only square brackets.
      * The function should return True if and only if there is a valid subsequence of brackets
      * where at least one bracket in the subsequence is nested.
      * 
      * is_nested('[[]]') ➞ True
      * is_nested('[]]]]]]][[[[[]') ➞ False
      * is_nested('[][]') ➞ False
      * is_nested('[]') ➞ False
      * is_nested('[[][]]') ➞ True
      * is_nested('[[]][[') ➞ True
      * '''

      * Complete the WORKING-STORAGE SECTION and the PROCEDURE DIVISION
      * Store the result in the RESULT variable and mark the end of your program with END PROGRAM

       PROCEDURE DIVISION USING LINKED-ITEMS.
           MOVE 0 TO RESULT
           MOVE 0 TO OPEN-COUNT
           MOVE 0 TO CLOSE-COUNT

           PERFORM VARYING I FROM 1 BY 1 UNTIL I > 100
               MOVE L-STRING(I:1) TO C-CHAR
               IF C-CHAR = SPACE
                   EXIT PERFORM
               ELSE
                   EVALUATE C-CHAR
                       WHEN '['
                           ADD 1 TO OPEN-COUNT
                       WHEN ']'
                           IF OPEN-COUNT >= 2
                               ADD 1 TO CLOSE-COUNT
                               IF CLOSE-COUNT >= 2
                                   MOVE 1 TO RESULT
                                   EXIT PERFORM
                               END-IF
                           END-IF
                       WHEN OTHER
                           CONTINUE
                   END-EVALUATE
               END-IF
           END-PERFORM

           GOBACK.
       END PROGRAM IS-NESTED.

IDENTIFICATION DIVISION.
       PROGRAM-ID. SUM-SQUARES.

       ENVIRONMENT DIVISION.
       
       INPUT-OUTPUT SECTION.

       DATA DIVISION.

       WORKING-STORAGE SECTION.
       
       
       01 WS-INDEX        PIC S9(4) COMP VALUE 1.
       01 WS-TEMP         COMP-2.
       01 WS-SQUARE       COMP-2.
       01 WS-SUM          PIC S9(10) VALUE 0.

       LINKAGE SECTION.

       01 LINKED-ITEMS.
           05 L-LST OCCURS 4 TIMES INDEXED BY NI COMP-2.
           05 RESULT PIC S9(10).

      * You are given a list of numbers.
      * You need to return the sum of squared numbers in the given list,
      * round each element in the list to the upper int(Ceiling) first.
      * Examples:
      * For lst = [1,3,5,7] the output should be 84
      * For lst = [1.4,4.2,0] the output should be 29
      * For lst = [-2.4,1,1] the output should be 6
      * 
      * 
      * 

      * Complete the WORKING-STORAGE SECTION and the PROCEDURE DIVISION
      * Store the result in the RESULT variable and mark the end of your program with END PROGRAM

       PROCEDURE DIVISION USING LINKED-ITEMS.
       
           MOVE 0 TO WS-SUM.
           PERFORM VARYING WS-INDEX FROM 1 BY 1 UNTIL WS-INDEX > 4
               MOVE L-LST(WS-INDEX) TO WS-TEMP
               COMPUTE WS-TEMP = FUNCTION INTEGER(WS-TEMP)
               IF L-LST(WS-INDEX) > WS-TEMP
                   ADD 1 TO WS-TEMP
               END-IF
               COMPUTE WS-SQUARE = WS-TEMP * WS-TEMP
               ADD WS-SQUARE TO WS-SUM
           END-PERFORM.

           MOVE WS-SUM TO RESULT.

           GOBACK.

       END PROGRAM SUM-SQUARES.

COBOL_canonical_solution:
IDENTIFICATION DIVISION.
       PROGRAM-ID. IS-LAST-CHAR-LETTER.

       ENVIRONMENT DIVISION.

       DATA DIVISION.

       WORKING-STORAGE SECTION.
       01 WS-TXT       PIC X(100).
       01 WS-LEN       PIC S9(3) VALUE 0.
       01 WS-LAST-CHAR PIC X(1).
       01 WS-RESULT    PIC 9 VALUE 0.
       01 WS-I         PIC S9(3).

       LINKAGE SECTION.
       01 LINKED-ITEMS.
           05 L-TXT  PIC X(100).
           05 RESULT PIC 9.

       PROCEDURE DIVISION USING LINKED-ITEMS.

           MOVE L-TXT TO WS-TXT

           MOVE 100 TO WS-LEN
           PERFORM VARYING WS-I FROM 100 BY -1 UNTIL WS-I < 1
               IF WS-TXT(WS-I:1) NOT = SPACE
                   MOVE WS-I TO WS-LEN
                   EXIT PERFORM
               END-IF
           END-PERFORM

           IF WS-LEN = 0
               MOVE 0 TO WS-RESULT
           ELSE
               MOVE WS-TXT(WS-LEN:1) TO WS-LAST-CHAR

               IF (WS-LAST-CHAR >= 'A' AND WS-LAST-CHAR <= 'Z') OR
                  (WS-LAST-CHAR >= 'a' AND WS-LAST-CHAR <= 'z')
                   
                   *> Check if the character is not part of a word
                   *> i.e., previous character is a space or it's the first character
                   IF WS-LEN = 1 OR WS-TXT(WS-LEN - 1:1) = SPACE
                       MOVE 1 TO WS-RESULT
                   ELSE
                       MOVE 0 TO WS-RESULT
                   END-IF
               ELSE
                   MOVE 0 TO WS-RESULT
               END-IF
           END-IF

           MOVE WS-RESULT TO RESULT
           EXIT PROGRAM.
       END PROGRAM IS-LAST-CHAR-LETTER.

IDENTIFICATION DIVISION.
       PROGRAM-ID. CAN-ARRANGE.

       ENVIRONMENT DIVISION.
       
       INPUT-OUTPUT SECTION.

       DATA DIVISION.

       WORKING-STORAGE SECTION.
       
       
       01 WS-IDX      PIC S9(10) VALUE 0.
       01 WS-PREV-IDX PIC S9(10) VALUE 0.

       LINKAGE SECTION.

       01 LINKED-ITEMS.
           05 L-ARR OCCURS 5 TIMES INDEXED BY NI PIC S9(10).
           05 RESULT PIC S9(10).

      * Create a function which returns the largest index of an element which
      * is not greater than or equal to the element immediately preceding it. If
      * no such element exists then return -1. The given array will not contain
      * duplicate values.
      * 
      * Examples:
      * can_arrange([1,2,4,3,5]) = 3
      * 

      * Complete the WORKING-STORAGE SECTION and the PROCEDURE DIVISION
      * Store the result in the RESULT variable and mark the end of your program with END PROGRAM

       PROCEDURE DIVISION USING LINKED-ITEMS.
           MOVE -1 TO RESULT.
           PERFORM VARYING WS-IDX FROM 2 BY 1 UNTIL WS-IDX > 5
               COMPUTE WS-PREV-IDX = WS-IDX - 1
               IF L-ARR(WS-IDX) < L-ARR(WS-PREV-IDX)
                   COMPUTE RESULT = WS-IDX - 1
               END-IF
           END-PERFORM.
           GOBACK.
       END PROGRAM CAN-ARRANGE.

IDENTIFICATION DIVISION.
       PROGRAM-ID. IS-EQUAL-TO-SUM-EVEN.

       ENVIRONMENT DIVISION.
       
       INPUT-OUTPUT SECTION.

       DATA DIVISION.

       WORKING-STORAGE SECTION.
       
       
       01 WS-VARIABLES.
           05 WS-REMAINDER PIC 9.

       LINKAGE SECTION.

       01 LINKED-ITEMS.
           05 L-N PIC S9(10).
           05 RESULT PIC 9.

      *>Evaluate whether the given number n can be written as the sum of exactly 4 positive even numbers
      *>Example
      *>is_equal_to_sum_even(4) == False
      *>is_equal_to_sum_even(6) == False
      *>is_equal_to_sum_even(8) == True
      *>

      *>Complete the WORKING-STORAGE SECTION and the PROCEDURE DIVISION
      *>Store the result in the RESULT variable and mark the end of your program with END PROGRAM

       PROCEDURE DIVISION USING LINKED-ITEMS.
       
       MAIN-LOGIC.
           MOVE 0 TO RESULT
           
           IF L-N < 8
               GO TO END-PROGRAM
           END-IF
           
           DIVIDE L-N BY 2 GIVING L-N REMAINDER WS-REMAINDER
           
           IF WS-REMAINDER = 0
               MOVE 1 TO RESULT
           END-IF
           
       END-PROGRAM.
           GOBACK.

COBOL_canonical_solution:
IDENTIFICATION DIVISION.
       PROGRAM-ID. SPECIAL-FACTORIAL.

       ENVIRONMENT DIVISION.

       DATA DIVISION.

       WORKING-STORAGE SECTION.
       01 WS-N        PIC S9(10) VALUE 0.
       01 WS-I        PIC S9(10) VALUE 0.
       01 WS-J        PIC S9(10) VALUE 0.
       01 WS-FACT     PIC S9(20) VALUE 1.
       01 WS-RESULT   PIC S9(20) VALUE 1.

       LINKAGE SECTION.
       01 LINKED-ITEMS.
           05 L-N      PIC S9(10).
           05 RESULT   PIC S9(20).

       PROCEDURE DIVISION USING LINKED-ITEMS.

           MOVE L-N TO WS-N

           MOVE 1 TO WS-RESULT

           PERFORM VARYING WS-I FROM WS-N BY -1 UNTIL WS-I < 1
               
               MOVE 1 TO WS-FACT
               PERFORM VARYING WS-J FROM 1 BY 1 UNTIL WS-J > WS-I
                   MULTIPLY WS-FACT BY WS-J GIVING WS-FACT
               END-PERFORM

               MULTIPLY WS-RESULT BY WS-FACT GIVING WS-RESULT
           END-PERFORM

           MOVE WS-RESULT TO RESULT

           EXIT PROGRAM.
       END PROGRAM SPECIAL-FACTORIAL.

IDENTIFICATION DIVISION.
       PROGRAM-ID. FILE-NAME-CHECK.

       ENVIRONMENT DIVISION.
       
       INPUT-OUTPUT SECTION.

       DATA DIVISION.

       WORKING-STORAGE SECTION.
       
       01 WS-VARIABLES.
           05 WS-DIGIT-COUNT PIC 9(3) VALUE 0.
           05 WS-DOT-COUNT PIC 9(3) VALUE 0.
           05 WS-DOT-POSITION PIC 9(3) VALUE 0.
           05 WS-FILE-LENGTH PIC 9(3) VALUE 0.
           05 WS-COUNTER PIC 9(3) VALUE 0.
           05 WS-CHAR PIC X(1).
           05 WS-BEFORE-DOT PIC X(99).
           05 WS-AFTER-DOT PIC X(10).
           05 WS-FIRST-CHAR PIC X(1).
           05 WS-VALID-FLAG PIC X(1) VALUE 'Y'.

       LINKAGE SECTION.

       01 LINKED-ITEMS.
           05 L-FILE-NAME PIC X(100).
           05 RESULT PIC X(100).

      *>Create a function which takes a string representing a file's name, and returns
      *>'Yes' if the the file's name is valid, and returns 'No' otherwise.
      *>A file's name is considered to be valid if and only if all the following conditions
      *>are met:
      *>- There should not be more than three digits ('0'-'9') in the file's name.
      *>- The file's name contains exactly one dot '.'
      *>- The substring before the dot should not be empty, and it starts with a letter from
      *>the latin alphapet ('a'-'z' and 'A'-'Z').
      *>- The substring after the dot should be one of these: ['txt', 'exe', 'dll']
      *>Examples:
      *>file_name_check("example.txt") # => 'Yes'
      *>file_name_check("1example.dll") # => 'No' (the name should start with a latin alphapet letter)
      *>

      *>Complete the WORKING-STORAGE SECTION and the PROCEDURE DIVISION
      *>Store the result in the RESULT variable and mark the end of your program with END PROGRAM

       PROCEDURE DIVISION USING LINKED-ITEMS.
       
       MAIN-LOGIC.
           INITIALIZE WS-VARIABLES
           MOVE 'Y' TO WS-VALID-FLAG
           
           PERFORM CALCULATE-LENGTH
           
           IF WS-FILE-LENGTH = 0
               MOVE 'No' TO RESULT
               GOBACK
           END-IF
           
           PERFORM VALIDATE-FILE-NAME
           
           IF WS-VALID-FLAG = 'Y'
               MOVE 'Yes' TO RESULT
           ELSE
               MOVE 'No' TO RESULT
           END-IF
           
           GOBACK.
           
       CALCULATE-LENGTH.
           MOVE 0 TO WS-FILE-LENGTH
           PERFORM VARYING WS-COUNTER FROM 1 BY 1 
               UNTIL WS-COUNTER > 100 OR L-FILE-NAME(WS-COUNTER:1) = SPACE
               ADD 1 TO WS-FILE-LENGTH
           END-PERFORM.
           
       VALIDATE-FILE-NAME.
           PERFORM VARYING WS-COUNTER FROM 1 BY 1 
               UNTIL WS-COUNTER > WS-FILE-LENGTH
               MOVE L-FILE-NAME(WS-COUNTER:1) TO WS-CHAR
               
               IF WS-CHAR >= '0' AND WS-CHAR <= '9'
                   ADD 1 TO WS-DIGIT-COUNT
               END-IF
               
               IF WS-CHAR = '.'
                   ADD 1 TO WS-DOT-COUNT
                   MOVE WS-COUNTER TO WS-DOT-POSITION
               END-IF
           END-PERFORM
           
           IF WS-DIGIT-COUNT > 3
               MOVE 'N' TO WS-VALID-FLAG
           END-IF
           
           IF WS-DOT-COUNT NOT = 1
               MOVE 'N' TO WS-VALID-FLAG
           END-IF
           
           IF WS-DOT-POSITION = 1
               MOVE 'N' TO WS-VALID-FLAG
           END-IF
           
           IF WS-DOT-POSITION > 0 AND WS-VALID-FLAG = 'Y'
               MOVE L-FILE-NAME(1:1) TO WS-FIRST-CHAR
               IF NOT ((WS-FIRST-CHAR >= 'a' AND WS-FIRST-CHAR <= 'z') OR
                       (WS-FIRST-CHAR >= 'A' AND WS-FIRST-CHAR <= 'Z'))
                   MOVE 'N' TO WS-VALID-FLAG
               END-IF
               
               COMPUTE WS-COUNTER = WS-DOT-POSITION + 1
               MOVE L-FILE-NAME(WS-COUNTER:WS-FILE-LENGTH - WS-DOT-POSITION) 
                   TO WS-AFTER-DOT
               
               IF WS-AFTER-DOT NOT = 'txt' AND 
                  WS-AFTER-DOT NOT = 'exe' AND 
                  WS-AFTER-DOT NOT = 'dll'
                   MOVE 'N' TO WS-VALID-FLAG
               END-IF
           END-IF.

       END PROGRAM FILE-NAME-CHECK.

      * file_name_check("1example.dll") # => 'No' (the name should start with a latin alphapet letter)

import java.lang.*;

    "Yes" if the the file's name is valid, and returns "No" otherwise.
    A file's name is considered to be valid if and only if all the following conditions
    are met:
    - There should not be more than three digits ('0'-'9') in the file's name.
    - The file's name contains exactly one dot '.'
    - The substring before the dot should not be empty, and it starts with a letter from
    the latin alphapet ('a'-'z' and 'A'-'Z').
    - The substring after the dot should be one of these: ["txt", "exe", "dll"]
    Examples:
    file_name_check("example.txt") # => "Yes"
    file_name_check("1example.dll") # => "No" (the name should start with a latin alphapet letter)
     */

Java_tests:
public class Main {
    public static void main(String[] args) {
        Solution s = new Solution();
        List<Boolean> correct = Arrays.asList(
                Objects.equals(s.filenameCheck("example.txt" ), "Yes" ),
                Objects.equals(s.filenameCheck("1example.dll" ), "No" ),
                Objects.equals(s.filenameCheck("s1sdf3.asd" ), "No" ),
                Objects.equals(s.filenameCheck("K.dll" ), "Yes" ),
                Objects.equals(s.filenameCheck("MY16FILE3.exe" ), "Yes" ),
                Objects.equals(s.filenameCheck("His12FILE94.exe" ), "No" ),
                Objects.equals(s.filenameCheck("_Y.txt" ), "No" ),
                Objects.equals(s.filenameCheck("?aREYA.exe" ), "No" ),
                Objects.equals(s.filenameCheck("/this_is_valid.dll" ), "No" ),
                Objects.equals(s.filenameCheck("this_is_valid.wow" ), "No" ),
                Objects.equals(s.filenameCheck("this_is_valid.txt" ), "Yes" ),
                Objects.equals(s.filenameCheck("this_is_valid.txtexe" ), "No" ),
                Objects.equals(s.filenameCheck("#this2_i4s_5valid.ten" ), "No" ),
                Objects.equals(s.filenameCheck("@this1_is6_valid.exe" ), "No" ),
                Objects.equals(s.filenameCheck("this_is_12valid.6exe4.txt" ), "No" ),
                Objects.equals(s.filenameCheck("all.exe.txt" ), "No" ),
                Objects.equals(s.filenameCheck("I563_No.exe" ), "Yes" ),
                Objects.equals(s.filenameCheck("Is3youfault.txt" ), "Yes" ),
                Objects.equals(s.filenameCheck("no_one#knows.dll" ), "Yes" ),
                Objects.equals(s.filenameCheck("1I563_Yes3.exe" ), "No" ),
                Objects.equals(s.filenameCheck("I563_Yes3.txtt" ), "No" ),
                Objects.equals(s.filenameCheck("final..txt" ), "No" ),
                Objects.equals(s.filenameCheck("final132" ), "No" ),
                Objects.equals(s.filenameCheck("_f4indsartal132." ), "No" )
        );
        if (correct.contains(false)) {
            throw new AssertionError();
        }
    }
}

IDENTIFICATION DIVISION.
       PROGRAM-ID. SUM-SQUARES.

       ENVIRONMENT DIVISION.
       
       INPUT-OUTPUT SECTION.

       DATA DIVISION.

       WORKING-STORAGE SECTION.
       
       
       
       01 WS-I             PIC S9(4).
       01 WS-ZI            PIC S9(10).
       01 WS-VALUE         PIC S9(18).
       01 WS-TEMP          PIC S9(18).
       01 WS-REM3          PIC S9(10).
       01 WS-REM4          PIC S9(10).

       LINKAGE SECTION.

       01 LINKED-ITEMS.
           05 L-LST OCCURS 3 TIMES INDEXED BY NI PIC S9(10).
           05 RESULT PIC S9(10).

      * 
      * This function will take a list of integers. For all entries in the list, the function shall square the integer entry if its index is a
      * multiple of 3 and will cube the integer entry if its index is a multiple of 4 and not a multiple of 3. The function will not
      * change the entries in the list whose indexes are not a multiple of 3 or 4. The function shall then return the sum of all entries.
      * 
      * Examples:
      * For lst = [1,2,3] the output should be 6
      * 

      * Complete the WORKING-STORAGE SECTION and the PROCEDURE DIVISION
      * Store the result in the RESULT variable and mark the end of your program with END PROGRAM

       PROCEDURE DIVISION USING LINKED-ITEMS.
           MOVE 0 TO RESULT
           PERFORM VARYING WS-I FROM 1 BY 1 UNTIL WS-I > 3
               COMPUTE WS-ZI = WS-I - 1
               DIVIDE WS-ZI BY 3 GIVING WS-TEMP REMAINDER WS-REM3
               DIVIDE WS-ZI BY 4 GIVING WS-TEMP REMAINDER WS-REM4

               IF WS-REM3 = 0
                   COMPUTE WS-VALUE = L-LST(WS-I) * L-LST(WS-I)
               ELSE
                   IF WS-REM4 = 0
                       COMPUTE WS-VALUE =
                           L-LST(WS-I) * L-LST(WS-I) * L-LST(WS-I)
                   ELSE
                       MOVE L-LST(WS-I) TO WS-VALUE
                   END-IF
               END-IF

               COMPUTE RESULT = RESULT + WS-VALUE
           END-PERFORM

           GOBACK.
       END PROGRAM SUM-SQUARES.

IDENTIFICATION DIVISION.
       PROGRAM-ID. WORDS-IN-SENTENCE.

       ENVIRONMENT DIVISION.
       
       INPUT-OUTPUT SECTION.

       DATA DIVISION.

       WORKING-STORAGE SECTION.
       
       
       01 WS-VARS.
           05 WS-I              PIC 9(3) VALUE 0.
           05 WS-LAST           PIC 9(3) VALUE 0.
           05 WS-FOUND-NONSPACE PIC X VALUE 'N'.
           05 WS-POS            PIC 9(3) VALUE 1.
           05 WS-WORD-START     PIC 9(3) VALUE 0.
           05 WS-WORD-LEN       PIC 9(3) VALUE 0.
           05 WS-IN-WORD        PIC X VALUE 'N'.
           05 WS-RESULT         PIC X(100) VALUE SPACES.
           05 WS-RESULT-POS     PIC 9(3) VALUE 1.
           05 WS-APPENDED-COUNT PIC 9(3) VALUE 0.
           05 WS-IS-PRIME       PIC X VALUE 'N'.
           05 WS-DIVISOR        PIC 9(3) VALUE 0.
           05 WS-REMAINDER      PIC 9(3) VALUE 0.
           05 WS-QUOTIENT       PIC 9(3) VALUE 0.
           05 WS-CHAR           PIC X VALUE SPACE.

       LINKAGE SECTION.

       01 LINKED-ITEMS.
           05 L-SENTENCE PIC X(100).
           05 RESULT PIC X(100).

      * 
      * You are given a string representing a sentence,
      * the sentence contains some words separated by a space,
      * and you have to return a string that contains the words from the original sentence,
      * whose lengths are prime numbers,
      * the order of the words in the new string should be the same as the original one.
      * 
      * Example 1:
      * Input: sentence = "This is a test"
      * Output: "is"
      * 
      * Example 2:
      * Input: sentence = "lets go for swimming"
      * Output: "go for"
      * 
      * Constraints:
      * * 1 <= len(sentence) <= 100
      * * sentence contains only letters
      * 

      * Complete the WORKING-STORAGE SECTION and the PROCEDURE DIVISION
      * Store the result in the RESULT variable and mark the end of your program with END PROGRAM

       PROCEDURE DIVISION USING LINKED-ITEMS.

           MOVE SPACES TO WS-RESULT.

      * Find last non-space character position to determine actual sentence length
           MOVE 'N' TO WS-FOUND-NONSPACE
           PERFORM VARYING WS-I FROM 100 BY -1
                   UNTIL WS-I = 0 OR WS-FOUND-NONSPACE = 'Y'
               IF L-SENTENCE(WS-I:1) NOT = SPACE
                   MOVE WS-I TO WS-LAST
                   MOVE 'Y' TO WS-FOUND-NONSPACE
               END-IF
           END-PERFORM

      * If sentence is empty or spaces, return empty result
           IF WS-LAST = 0
               MOVE WS-RESULT TO RESULT
               GOBACK
           END-IF

      * Parse words and collect those with prime lengths
           MOVE 1 TO WS-POS
           MOVE 'N' TO WS-IN-WORD
           MOVE 0 TO WS-APPENDED-COUNT
           MOVE 1 TO WS-RESULT-POS

           PERFORM UNTIL WS-POS > WS-LAST
               MOVE L-SENTENCE(WS-POS:1) TO WS-CHAR
               IF WS-CHAR = SPACE
                   IF WS-IN-WORD = 'Y'
                       PERFORM CHECK-AND-APPEND
                       MOVE 'N' TO WS-IN-WORD
                       MOVE 0 TO WS-WORD-LEN
                       MOVE 0 TO WS-WORD-START
                   END-IF
               ELSE
                   IF WS-IN-WORD = 'N'
                       MOVE 'Y' TO WS-IN-WORD
                       MOVE WS-POS TO WS-WORD-START
                       MOVE 1 TO WS-WORD-LEN
                   ELSE
                       ADD 1 TO WS-WORD-LEN
                   END-IF
               END-IF
               ADD 1 TO WS-POS
           END-PERFORM

      * Handle a trailing word (no space at the end)
           IF WS-IN-WORD = 'Y'
               PERFORM CHECK-AND-APPEND
           END-IF

           MOVE WS-RESULT TO RESULT
           GOBACK.

       CHECK-AND-APPEND.
           PERFORM PRIME-CHECK
           IF WS-IS-PRIME = 'Y'
               IF WS-APPENDED-COUNT > 0
                   MOVE SPACE TO WS-RESULT(WS-RESULT-POS:1)
                   ADD 1 TO WS-RESULT-POS
               END-IF
               MOVE L-SENTENCE(WS-WORD-START:WS-WORD-LEN)
                   TO WS-RESULT(WS-RESULT-POS:WS-WORD-LEN)
               ADD 1 TO WS-APPENDED-COUNT
               ADD WS-WORD-LEN TO WS-RESULT-POS
           END-IF
           EXIT.

       PRIME-CHECK.
           IF WS-WORD-LEN <= 1
               MOVE 'N' TO WS-IS-PRIME
           ELSE
               MOVE 'Y' TO WS-IS-PRIME
               PERFORM VARYING WS-DIVISOR FROM 2 BY 1
                       UNTIL WS-DIVISOR >= WS-WORD-LEN OR WS-IS-PRIME = 'N'
                   DIVIDE WS-DIVISOR INTO WS-WORD-LEN
                       GIVING WS-QUOTIENT REMAINDER WS-REMAINDER
                   IF WS-REMAINDER = 0
                       MOVE 'N' TO WS-IS-PRIME
                   END-IF
               END-PERFORM
           END-IF
           EXIT.

       END PROGRAM WORDS-IN-SENTENCE.

COBOL_canonical_solution:
IDENTIFICATION DIVISION.
       PROGRAM-ID. SIMPLIFY.

       ENVIRONMENT DIVISION.
       
       INPUT-OUTPUT SECTION.

       DATA DIVISION.

       WORKING-STORAGE SECTION.
       
       
       01 WS-X-NUM PIC 9(10).
       01 WS-X-DEN PIC 9(10).
       01 WS-N-NUM PIC 9(10).
       01 WS-N-DEN PIC 9(10).
       01 WS-RESULT-NUM PIC 9(20).
       01 WS-RESULT-DEN PIC 9(20).
       01 WS-REMAINDER PIC 9(20).
       01 WS-SLASH-POS PIC 9(3).
       01 WS-I PIC 9(3).
       01 WS-TEMP-STR PIC X(100).

       LINKAGE SECTION.

       01 LINKED-ITEMS.
           05 L-X PIC X(100).
           05 L-N PIC X(100).
           05 RESULT PIC 9.

      *>Your task is to implement a function that will simplify the expression
      *>x * n. The function returns True if x * n evaluates to a whole number and False
      *>otherwise. Both x and n, are string representation of a fraction, and have the following format,
      *><numerator>/<denominator> where both numerator and denominator are positive whole numbers.
      *>
      *>You can assume that x, and n are valid fractions, and do not have zero as denominator.
      *>
      *>simplify("1/5", "5/1") = True
      *>simplify("1/6", "2/1") = False
      *>simplify("7/10", "10/2") = False
      *>

      *>Complete the WORKING-STORAGE SECTION and the PROCEDURE DIVISION
      *>Store the result in the RESULT variable and mark the end of your program with END PROGRAM

       PROCEDURE DIVISION USING LINKED-ITEMS.
       
       MAIN-LOGIC.
           MOVE 0 TO RESULT
           
      *>   Parse first fraction (x)
           PERFORM FIND-SLASH-IN-X
           MOVE L-X(1:WS-SLASH-POS - 1) TO WS-TEMP-STR
           MOVE FUNCTION NUMVAL(WS-TEMP-STR) TO WS-X-NUM
           COMPUTE WS-I = WS-SLASH-POS + 1
           MOVE L-X(WS-I:) TO WS-TEMP-STR
           MOVE FUNCTION NUMVAL(WS-TEMP-STR) TO WS-X-DEN
           
      *>   Parse second fraction (n)
           PERFORM FIND-SLASH-IN-N
           MOVE L-N(1:WS-SLASH-POS - 1) TO WS-TEMP-STR
           MOVE FUNCTION NUMVAL(WS-TEMP-STR) TO WS-N-NUM
           COMPUTE WS-I = WS-SLASH-POS + 1
           MOVE L-N(WS-I:) TO WS-TEMP-STR
           MOVE FUNCTION NUMVAL(WS-TEMP-STR) TO WS-N-DEN
           
      *>   Multiply fractions: (x_num * n_num) / (x_den * n_den)
           COMPUTE WS-RESULT-NUM = WS-X-NUM * WS-N-NUM
           COMPUTE WS-RESULT-DEN = WS-X-DEN * WS-N-DEN
           
      *>   Check if result is a whole number
           DIVIDE WS-RESULT-NUM BY WS-RESULT-DEN 
               GIVING WS-I REMAINDER WS-REMAINDER
           
           IF WS-REMAINDER = 0
               MOVE 1 TO RESULT
           ELSE
               MOVE 0 TO RESULT
           END-IF
           
           GOBACK.
           
       FIND-SLASH-IN-X.
           PERFORM VARYING WS-I FROM 1 BY 1 
               UNTIL WS-I > 100 OR L-X(WS-I:1) = '/'
           END-PERFORM
           MOVE WS-I TO WS-SLASH-POS.
           
       FIND-SLASH-IN-N.
           PERFORM VARYING WS-I FROM 1 BY 1 
               UNTIL WS-I > 100 OR L-N(WS-I:1) = '/'
           END-PERFORM
           MOVE WS-I TO WS-SLASH-POS.

IDENTIFICATION DIVISION.
       PROGRAM-ID. ORDER-BY-POINTS.

       ENVIRONMENT DIVISION.

       INPUT-OUTPUT SECTION.

       DATA DIVISION.

       WORKING-STORAGE SECTION.
       77 WS-I        PIC 9(2) VALUE 1.
       77 WS-J        PIC 9(2) VALUE 1.
       77 WS-K        PIC 9(2) VALUE 1.
       77 WS-LEN      PIC 9(3).
       77 WS-DIGIT    PIC 9(2).
       77 WS-TEMP     PIC S9(10).
       77 WS-NUM      PIC S9(10).
       77 WS-SUM      PIC S9(10) OCCURS 5 TIMES INDEXED BY NI.

       LINKAGE SECTION.
       01 LINKED-ITEMS.
           05 L-NUMS  OCCURS 5 TIMES INDEXED BY NI PIC S9(10).
           05 RESULT  OCCURS 5 TIMES INDEXED BY NJ PIC S9(10).

       PROCEDURE DIVISION USING LINKED-ITEMS.

       *> Step 1: Calculate sum of digits for each number (consider sign)
           PERFORM VARYING WS-I FROM 1 BY 1 UNTIL WS-I > 5
               MOVE L-NUMS(WS-I) TO WS-NUM
               MOVE 0 TO WS-SUM(WS-I)

               IF WS-NUM >= 0
                   *> Positive number: sum digits normally
                   PERFORM UNTIL WS-NUM = 0
                       MOVE FUNCTION MOD(WS-NUM 10) TO WS-DIGIT
                       ADD WS-DIGIT TO WS-SUM(WS-I)
                       COMPUTE WS-NUM = WS-NUM / 10
                   END-PERFORM
               ELSE
                   *> Negative number: subtract first digit, add remaining
                   COMPUTE WS-NUM = WS-NUM * -1
                   MOVE 1 TO WS-K
                   *> Get first digit
                   PERFORM UNTIL WS-NUM < 10
                       COMPUTE WS-NUM = WS-NUM / 10
                   END-PERFORM
                   MOVE WS-NUM TO WS-DIGIT
                   COMPUTE WS-SUM(WS-I) = 0 - WS-DIGIT

                   *> Now add remaining digits
                   MOVE L-NUMS(WS-I) TO WS-NUM
                   COMPUTE WS-NUM = WS-NUM * -1
                   PERFORM UNTIL WS-NUM < 10
                       MOVE FUNCTION MOD(WS-NUM 10) TO WS-DIGIT
                       ADD WS-DIGIT TO WS-SUM(WS-I)
                       COMPUTE WS-NUM = WS-NUM / 10
                   END-PERFORM
               END-IF
           END-PERFORM

       *> Step 2: Sort numbers by sum of digits using bubble sort (stable)
           PERFORM VARYING WS-I FROM 1 BY 1 UNTIL WS-I >= 5
               MOVE WS-I TO WS-J
               PERFORM UNTIL WS-J >= 5
                   ADD 1 TO WS-J
                   IF WS-SUM(WS-I) > WS-SUM(WS-J)
                       *> Swap sums
                       MOVE WS-SUM(WS-I) TO WS-TEMP
                       MOVE WS-SUM(WS-J) TO WS-SUM(WS-I)
                       MOVE WS-TEMP TO WS-SUM(WS-J)

                       *> Swap numbers
                       MOVE L-NUMS(WS-I) TO WS-TEMP
                       MOVE L-NUMS(WS-J) TO L-NUMS(WS-I)
                       MOVE WS-TEMP TO L-NUMS(WS-J)
                   END-IF
               END-PERFORM
           END-PERFORM

       *> Step 3: Move sorted numbers to RESULT
           PERFORM VARYING WS-I FROM 1 BY 1 UNTIL WS-I > 5
               MOVE L-NUMS(WS-I) TO RESULT(WS-I)
           END-PERFORM

           EXIT PROGRAM.
       END PROGRAM ORDER-BY-POINTS.

COBOL_canonical_solution:
IDENTIFICATION DIVISION.
       PROGRAM-ID. SPECIALFILTER.

       ENVIRONMENT DIVISION.
       
       INPUT-OUTPUT SECTION.

       DATA DIVISION.

       WORKING-STORAGE SECTION.
       
       
       
       01 I                PIC 9(4)   VALUE 0.
       01 WS-COUNT         PIC S9(10) VALUE 0.
       01 WS-N             PIC S9(10) VALUE 0.
       01 ABS-N            PIC 9(10)  VALUE 0.
       01 TEMP             PIC 9(10)  VALUE 0.
       01 LAST-DIGIT       PIC 9      VALUE 0.
       01 FIRST-DIGIT      PIC 9      VALUE 0.
       01 Q                PIC 9(10)  VALUE 0.
       01 R                PIC 9      VALUE 0.
       01 FIRST-REM        PIC 9      VALUE 0.
       01 LAST-REM         PIC 9      VALUE 0.

       LINKAGE SECTION.

       01 LINKED-ITEMS.
           05 L-NUMS OCCURS 6 TIMES INDEXED BY NI PIC S9(10).
           05 RESULT PIC S9(10).

      * Write a function that takes an array of numbers as input and returns
      * the number of elements in the array that are greater than 10 and both
      * first and last digits of a number are odd (1, 3, 5, 7, 9).
      * For example:
      * specialFilter([15, -73, 14, -15, 8, 9]) => 1
      * specialFilter([33, -2, -3, 45, 21, 109]) => 2
      * 

      * Complete the WORKING-STORAGE SECTION and the PROCEDURE DIVISION
      * Store the result in the RESULT variable and mark the end of your program with END PROGRAM

       PROCEDURE DIVISION USING LINKED-ITEMS.
           MOVE 0 TO RESULT
           MOVE 0 TO WS-COUNT

           PERFORM VARYING I FROM 1 BY 1 UNTIL I > 6
               MOVE L-NUMS(I) TO WS-N
               IF WS-N > 10
                  IF WS-N < 0
                     COMPUTE ABS-N = 0 - WS-N
                  ELSE
                     MOVE WS-N TO ABS-N
                  END-IF

                  MOVE ABS-N TO TEMP

                  DIVIDE ABS-N BY 10 GIVING Q REMAINDER LAST-DIGIT

                  PERFORM UNTIL TEMP < 10
                      DIVIDE TEMP BY 10 GIVING TEMP REMAINDER R
                  END-PERFORM

                  MOVE TEMP TO FIRST-DIGIT

                  DIVIDE FIRST-DIGIT BY 2 GIVING Q REMAINDER FIRST-REM
                  DIVIDE LAST-DIGIT  BY 2 GIVING Q REMAINDER LAST-REM

                  IF FIRST-REM = 1 AND LAST-REM = 1
                      ADD 1 TO WS-COUNT
                  END-IF
               END-IF
           END-PERFORM

           MOVE WS-COUNT TO RESULT
           GOBACK.
       END PROGRAM SPECIALFILTER.

IDENTIFICATION DIVISION.
       PROGRAM-ID. GET-MAX-TRIPLES.

       ENVIRONMENT DIVISION.
       
       INPUT-OUTPUT SECTION.

       DATA DIVISION.

       WORKING-STORAGE SECTION.
       
       
       01 WS-VARIABLES.
           05 WS-I PIC S9(10).
           05 WS-J PIC S9(10).
           05 WS-K PIC S9(10).
           05 WS-A-I PIC S9(10).
           05 WS-A-J PIC S9(10).
           05 WS-A-K PIC S9(10).
           05 WS-SUM PIC S9(10).
           05 WS-REMAINDER PIC S9(10).
           05 WS-COUNT PIC S9(10).

       LINKAGE SECTION.

       01 LINKED-ITEMS.
           05 L-N PIC S9(10).
           05 RESULT PIC S9(10).

      *>
      *>You are given a positive integer n. You have to create an integer array a of length n.
      *>For each i (1 ≤ i ≤ n), the value of a[i] = i * i - i + 1.
      *>Return the number of triples (a[i], a[j], a[k]) of a where i < j < k,
      *>and a[i] + a[j] + a[k] is a multiple of 3.
      *>
      *>Example :
      *>Input: n = 5
      *>Output: 1
      *>Explanation:
      *>a = [1, 3, 7, 13, 21]
      *>The only valid triple is (1, 7, 13).
      *>

      *>Complete the WORKING-STORAGE SECTION and the PROCEDURE DIVISION
      *>Store the result in the RESULT variable and mark the end of your program with END PROGRAM

       PROCEDURE DIVISION USING LINKED-ITEMS.
       
       MAIN-LOGIC.
           MOVE ZERO TO WS-COUNT
           
           PERFORM VARYING WS-I FROM 1 BY 1 UNTIL WS-I > L-N
               PERFORM VARYING WS-J FROM WS-I BY 1 UNTIL WS-J > L-N
                   IF WS-J > WS-I
                       PERFORM VARYING WS-K FROM WS-J BY 1 UNTIL WS-K > L-N
                           IF WS-K > WS-J
                               COMPUTE WS-A-I = WS-I * WS-I - WS-I + 1
                               COMPUTE WS-A-J = WS-J * WS-J - WS-J + 1
                               COMPUTE WS-A-K = WS-K * WS-K - WS-K + 1
                               COMPUTE WS-SUM = WS-A-I + WS-A-J + WS-A-K
                               DIVIDE WS-SUM BY 3 GIVING WS-SUM REMAINDER WS-REMAINDER
                               IF WS-REMAINDER = ZERO
                                   ADD 1 TO WS-COUNT
                               END-IF
                           END-IF
                       END-PERFORM
                   END-IF
               END-PERFORM
           END-PERFORM
           
           MOVE WS-COUNT TO RESULT
           
           GOBACK.

           05 WS-END-POS PIC 9(2) VALUE 0.
           05 WS-RESULT-COUNT PIC 9(2) VALUE 0.

           05 WS-PLANET1-FOUND PIC X VALUE 'N'.
           05 WS-PLANET2-FOUND PIC X VALUE 'N'.

       LINKAGE SECTION.

       01 LINKED-ITEMS.
           05 L-PLANET1 PIC X(100).
           05 L-PLANET2 PIC X(100).
           05 RESULT OCCURS 100 TIMES INDEXED BY NI PIC X(100).

      *>'''
      *>There are eight planets in our solar system: the closerst to the Sun
      *>is Mercury, the next one is Venus, then Earth, Mars, Jupiter, Saturn,
      *>Uranus, Neptune.
      *>Write a function that takes two planet names as strings planet1 and planet2.
      *>The function should return a tuple containing all planets whose orbits are
      *>located between the orbit of planet1 and the orbit of planet2, sorted by
      *>the proximity to the sun.
      *>The function should return an empty tuple if planet1 or planet2
      *>are not correct planet names.
      *>Examples
      *>bf("Jupiter", "Neptune") ==> ("Saturn", "Uranus")
      *>bf("Earth", "Mercury") ==> ("Venus")
      *>bf("Mercury", "Uranus") ==> ("Venus", "Earth", "Mars", "Jupiter", "Saturn")
      *>'''

      *>Complete the WORKING-STORAGE SECTION and the PROCEDURE DIVISION
      *>Store the result in the RESULT variable and mark the end of your program with END PROGRAM

       PROCEDURE DIVISION USING LINKED-ITEMS.
       
       MAIN-LOGIC.
           PERFORM INITIALIZE-PLANETS
           PERFORM FIND-PLANET-POSITIONS
           PERFORM BUILD-RESULT
           GOBACK.
       
       INITIALIZE-PLANETS.
           MOVE 'Mercury' TO WS-PLANET(1)
           MOVE 'Venus' TO WS-PLANET(2)
           MOVE 'Earth' TO WS-PLANET(3)
           MOVE 'Mars' TO WS-PLANET(4)
           MOVE 'Jupiter' TO WS-PLANET(5)
           MOVE 'Saturn' TO WS-PLANET(6)
           MOVE 'Uranus' TO WS-PLANET(7)
           MOVE 'Neptune' TO WS-PLANET(8).
       
       FIND-PLANET-POSITIONS.
           PERFORM VARYING WS-I FROM 1 BY 1 UNTIL WS-I > 8
               IF FUNCTION TRIM(WS-PLANET(WS-I)) = FUNCTION TRIM(L-PLANET1)
                   MOVE WS-I TO WS-PLANET1-POS
                   MOVE 'Y' TO WS-PLANET1-FOUND
               END-IF
               IF FUNCTION TRIM(WS-PLANET(WS-I)) = FUNCTION TRIM(L-PLANET2)
                   MOVE WS-I TO WS-PLANET2-POS
                   MOVE 'Y' TO WS-PLANET2-FOUND
               END-IF
           END-PERFORM.
       
       BUILD-RESULT.
           MOVE 0 TO WS-RESULT-COUNT
           
           IF WS-PLANET1-FOUND = 'N' OR WS-PLANET2-FOUND = 'N'
               EXIT PARAGRAPH
           END-IF
           
           IF WS-PLANET1-POS < WS-PLANET2-POS
               COMPUTE WS-START-POS = WS-PLANET1-POS + 1
               MOVE WS-PLANET2-POS TO WS-END-POS
           ELSE
               COMPUTE WS-START-POS = WS-PLANET2-POS + 1
               MOVE WS-PLANET1-POS TO WS-END-POS
           END-IF
           
           PERFORM VARYING WS-I FROM WS-START-POS BY 1 
               UNTIL WS-I >= WS-END-POS
               ADD 1 TO WS-RESULT-COUNT
               MOVE WS-PLANET(WS-I) TO RESULT(WS-RESULT-COUNT)
           END-PERFORM.

       GOBACK

IDENTIFICATION DIVISION.
       PROGRAM-ID. SORTED-LIST-SUM.

       ENVIRONMENT DIVISION.

       DATA DIVISION.

       WORKING-STORAGE SECTION.
       77 WS-I        PIC 9(2) VALUE 1.
       77 WS-J        PIC 9(2) VALUE 1.
       77 WS-K        PIC 9(2) VALUE 1.
       77 WS-LEN-I    PIC 9(2).
       77 WS-LEN-J    PIC 9(2).
       77 WS-COUNT    PIC 9(2) VALUE 0.
       77 WS-STR-I    PIC X(100).
       77 WS-STR-J    PIC X(100).

       LINKAGE SECTION.
       01 LINKED-ITEMS.
           05 L-LST  OCCURS 4 TIMES INDEXED BY NI PIC X(4).
           05 RESULT OCCURS 100 TIMES INDEXED BY NJ PIC X(100).

       PROCEDURE DIVISION USING LINKED-ITEMS.

       *> Step 1: Keep only even-length strings
           MOVE 0 TO WS-COUNT
           PERFORM VARYING WS-I FROM 1 BY 1 UNTIL WS-I > 4
               MOVE L-LST(WS-I) TO WS-STR-I
               COMPUTE WS-LEN-I = FUNCTION LENGTH(FUNCTION TRIM(WS-STR-I))
               IF FUNCTION MOD(WS-LEN-I 2) = 0
                   ADD 1 TO WS-COUNT
                   MOVE WS-STR-I TO RESULT(WS-COUNT)
               END-IF
           END-PERFORM

       *> Step 2: Sort by length ascending, then alphabetically
           PERFORM VARYING WS-I FROM 1 BY 1 UNTIL WS-I >= WS-COUNT
               MOVE WS-I TO WS-K
               COMPUTE WS-J = WS-I + 1
               PERFORM VARYING WS-J FROM WS-J BY 1 UNTIL WS-J > WS-COUNT
                   MOVE RESULT(WS-I) TO WS-STR-I
                   MOVE RESULT(WS-J) TO WS-STR-J

                   COMPUTE WS-LEN-I = FUNCTION LENGTH(FUNCTION TRIM(WS-STR-I))
                   COMPUTE WS-LEN-J = FUNCTION LENGTH(FUNCTION TRIM(WS-STR-J))

                   IF WS-LEN-I > WS-LEN-J
                       *> Swap
                       MOVE WS-STR-I TO RESULT(WS-J)
                       MOVE WS-STR-J TO RESULT(WS-I)
                   ELSE IF WS-LEN-I = WS-LEN-J
                       IF WS-STR-I > WS-STR-J
                           *> Swap alphabetically
                           MOVE WS-STR-I TO RESULT(WS-J)
                           MOVE WS-STR-J TO RESULT(WS-I)
                       END-IF
                   END-IF
               END-PERFORM
           END-PERFORM

           EXIT PROGRAM.
       END PROGRAM SORTED-LIST-SUM.

       MOVE "b" TO L-LST(2)
       MOVE "c" TO L-LST(3)

        END-IF

COBOL_canonical_solution:
IDENTIFICATION DIVISION.
       PROGRAM-ID. X-OR-Y.

       ENVIRONMENT DIVISION.
       
       INPUT-OUTPUT SECTION.

       DATA DIVISION.

       WORKING-STORAGE SECTION.
       
       
       01 WS-VARIABLES.
           05 WS-I PIC S9(10).
           05 WS-SQRT-N PIC S9(10).
           05 WS-REMAINDER PIC S9(10).
           05 WS-IS-PRIME PIC X VALUE 'Y'.

       LINKAGE SECTION.

       01 LINKED-ITEMS.
           05 L-N PIC S9(10).
           05 L-X PIC S9(10).
           05 L-Y PIC S9(10).
           05 RESULT PIC S9(10).

      *>A simple program which should return the value of x if n is
      *>a prime number and should return the value of y otherwise.
      *>
      *>Examples:
      *>for x_or_y(7, 34, 12) == 34
      *>for x_or_y(15, 8, 5) == 5
      *>
      *>

      *>Complete the WORKING-STORAGE SECTION and the PROCEDURE DIVISION
      *>Store the result in the RESULT variable and mark the end of your program with END PROGRAM

       PROCEDURE DIVISION USING LINKED-ITEMS.
       
       MAIN-LOGIC.
           MOVE 'Y' TO WS-IS-PRIME
           
           IF L-N <= 1
               MOVE 'N' TO WS-IS-PRIME
           ELSE
               IF L-N = 2
                   MOVE 'Y' TO WS-IS-PRIME
               ELSE
                   IF FUNCTION MOD(L-N, 2) = 0
                       MOVE 'N' TO WS-IS-PRIME
                   ELSE
                       COMPUTE WS-SQRT-N = FUNCTION SQRT(L-N)
                       PERFORM VARYING WS-I FROM 3 BY 2 
                           UNTIL WS-I > WS-SQRT-N OR WS-IS-PRIME = 'N'
                           COMPUTE WS-REMAINDER = FUNCTION MOD(L-N, WS-I)
                           IF WS-REMAINDER = 0
                               MOVE 'N' TO WS-IS-PRIME
                           END-IF
                       END-PERFORM
                   END-IF
               END-IF
           END-IF
           
           IF WS-IS-PRIME = 'Y'
               MOVE L-X TO RESULT
           ELSE
               MOVE L-Y TO RESULT
           END-IF.
           
           GOBACK.

COBOL_canonical_solution:
IDENTIFICATION DIVISION.
       PROGRAM-ID. DOUBLE-THE-DIFFERENCE.

       ENVIRONMENT DIVISION.
       
       INPUT-OUTPUT SECTION.

       DATA DIVISION.

       WORKING-STORAGE SECTION.
       
       01 WS-VARIABLES.
           05 WS-INDEX PIC 9(3) COMP.
           05 WS-CURRENT-NUM COMP-2.
           05 WS-INTEGER-PART PIC S9(10).
           05 WS-DECIMAL-PART COMP-2.
           05 WS-SQUARE COMP-2.
           05 WS-SUM PIC S9(10) VALUE ZERO.

       LINKAGE SECTION.

       01 LINKED-ITEMS.
           05 L-LST OCCURS 3 TIMES INDEXED BY NI COMP-2.
           05 RESULT PIC S9(10).

      *>'''
      *>Given a list of numbers, return the sum of squares of the numbers
      *>in the list that are odd. Ignore numbers that are negative or not integers.
      *>
      *>double_the_difference([1, 3, 2]) == 1 + 9 + 0 = 10
      *>double_the_difference([-1, -2, 0]) == 0
      *>
      *>
      *>If the input list is empty, return 0.
      *>'''

      *>Complete the WORKING-STORAGE SECTION and the PROCEDURE DIVISION
      *>Store the result in the RESULT variable and mark the end of your program with END PROGRAM

       PROCEDURE DIVISION USING LINKED-ITEMS.
       
       MAIN-LOGIC.
           MOVE ZERO TO RESULT
           MOVE ZERO TO WS-SUM
           
           PERFORM VARYING WS-INDEX FROM 1 BY 1 
               UNTIL WS-INDEX > 3
               
               MOVE L-LST(WS-INDEX) TO WS-CURRENT-NUM
               
               IF WS-CURRENT-NUM > ZERO
                   MOVE WS-CURRENT-NUM TO WS-INTEGER-PART
                   COMPUTE WS-DECIMAL-PART = 
                       WS-CURRENT-NUM - WS-INTEGER-PART
                   
                   IF WS-DECIMAL-PART = ZERO
                       DIVIDE WS-INTEGER-PART BY 2 
                           GIVING WS-INTEGER-PART 
                           REMAINDER WS-INTEGER-PART
                       
                       IF WS-INTEGER-PART = 1
                           COMPUTE WS-SQUARE = 
                               WS-CURRENT-NUM * WS-CURRENT-NUM
                           ADD WS-SQUARE TO WS-SUM
                       END-IF
                   END-IF
               END-IF
           END-PERFORM
           
           MOVE WS-SUM TO RESULT
           
           GOBACK.

IDENTIFICATION DIVISION.
       PROGRAM-ID. COMPARE.

       ENVIRONMENT DIVISION.
       
       INPUT-OUTPUT SECTION.

       DATA DIVISION.

       WORKING-STORAGE SECTION.
       
       01 WS-COUNTER PIC S9(10) VALUE 1.
       01 WS-DIFFERENCE PIC S9(10).
       01 WS-GAME-VALUE PIC S9(10).
       01 WS-GUESS-VALUE PIC S9(10).

       LINKAGE SECTION.

       01 LINKED-ITEMS.
           05 L-GAME OCCURS 6 TIMES INDEXED BY NI PIC S9(10).
           05 L-GUESS OCCURS 6 TIMES INDEXED BY NJ PIC S9(10).
           05 RESULT OCCURS 100 TIMES INDEXED BY NK PIC S9(10).

      *>I think we all remember that feeling when the result of some long-awaited
      *>event is finally known. The feelings and thoughts you have at that moment are
      *>definitely worth noting down and comparing.
      *>Your task is to determine if a person correctly guessed the results of a number of matches.
      *>You are given two arrays of scores and guesses of equal length, where each index shows a match.
      *>Return an array of the same length denoting how far off each guess was. If they have guessed correctly,
      *>the value is 0, and if not, the value is the absolute difference between the guess and the score.
      *>
      *>
      *>example:
      *>
      *>compare([1,2,3,4,5,1],[1,2,3,4,2,-2]) -> [0,0,0,0,3,3]
      *>compare([0,5,0,0,0,4],[4,1,1,0,0,-2]) -> [4,4,1,0,0,6]
      *>

      *>Complete the WORKING-STORAGE SECTION and the PROCEDURE DIVISION
      *>Store the result in the RESULT variable and mark the end of your program with END PROGRAM

       PROCEDURE DIVISION USING LINKED-ITEMS.

       MAIN-PROCESS.
           SET NI TO 1
           SET NJ TO 1
           SET NK TO 1
           
           PERFORM VARYING WS-COUNTER FROM 1 BY 1 UNTIL WS-COUNTER > 6
               MOVE L-GAME(WS-COUNTER) TO WS-GAME-VALUE
               MOVE L-GUESS(WS-COUNTER) TO WS-GUESS-VALUE
               
               COMPUTE WS-DIFFERENCE = WS-GAME-VALUE - WS-GUESS-VALUE
               
               IF WS-DIFFERENCE < 0
                   COMPUTE WS-DIFFERENCE = WS-DIFFERENCE * -1
               END-IF
               
               MOVE WS-DIFFERENCE TO RESULT(WS-COUNTER)
           END-PERFORM.

       GOBACK.

COBOL_canonical_solution:
IDENTIFICATION DIVISION.
       PROGRAM-ID. STRONGEST-EXTENSION.

       DATA DIVISION.
       WORKING-STORAGE SECTION.

       77 WS-I              PIC 9(2) VALUE 1.
       77 WS-BEST-IDX       PIC 9(2) VALUE 1.
       77 WS-BEST-STR       PIC S9(4) VALUE -9999.
       77 WS-CUR-STR        PIC S9(4).
       77 WS-LEN            PIC 9(3).
       77 WS-K              PIC 9(3).
       77 WS-CHAR           PIC X.
       77 WS-UP             PIC 9 VALUE 0.
       77 WS-LOW            PIC 9 VALUE 0.

       LINKAGE SECTION.
       01 LINKED-ITEMS.
           05 L-CLASS-NAME       PIC X(100).
           05 L-EXTENSIONS       OCCURS 4 TIMES INDEXED BY NI PIC X(100).
           05 RESULT             PIC X(100).

       PROCEDURE DIVISION USING LINKED-ITEMS.
       MAIN-LOGIC.

           PERFORM VARYING WS-I FROM 1 BY 1 UNTIL WS-I > 4

               MOVE 0 TO WS-UP
               MOVE 0 TO WS-LOW
               MOVE FUNCTION LENGTH(FUNCTION TRIM(L-EXTENSIONS(WS-I))) TO WS-LEN

               PERFORM VARYING WS-K FROM 1 BY 1 UNTIL WS-K > WS-LEN
                   MOVE L-EXTENSIONS(WS-I)(WS-K:1) TO WS-CHAR
                   IF WS-CHAR >= "A" AND WS-CHAR <= "Z"
                       ADD 1 TO WS-UP
                   ELSE
                       IF WS-CHAR >= "a" AND WS-CHAR <= "z"
                           ADD 1 TO WS-LOW
                       END-IF
                   END-IF
               END-PERFORM

               COMPUTE WS-CUR-STR = WS-UP - WS-LOW

               *> Choose strongest (tie → keep earlier)
               IF WS-CUR-STR > WS-BEST-STR
                   MOVE WS-CUR-STR TO WS-BEST-STR
                   MOVE WS-I TO WS-BEST-IDX
               END-IF

           END-PERFORM

           MOVE L-CLASS-NAME TO RESULT
           
           MOVE FUNCTION LENGTH(FUNCTION TRIM(RESULT TRAILING)) TO WS-K
            ADD 1 TO WS-K
            
            STRING
                "." DELIMITED BY SIZE
                L-EXTENSIONS(WS-BEST-IDX) DELIMITED BY SIZE
                INTO RESULT WITH POINTER WS-K
            END-STRING

           EXIT PROGRAM.
       END PROGRAM STRONGEST-EXTENSION.

COBOL_canonical_solution:
IDENTIFICATION DIVISION.
       PROGRAM-ID. CYCPATTERN-CHECK.

       ENVIRONMENT DIVISION.
       
       INPUT-OUTPUT SECTION.

       DATA DIVISION.

       WORKING-STORAGE SECTION.
       
       
       
       77 WA                PIC X(100) VALUE SPACES.
       77 WB                PIC X(100) VALUE SPACES.
       77 ROT               PIC X(100) VALUE SPACES.
       77 LEN-A             PIC 9(4)   VALUE 0.
       77 LEN-B             PIC 9(4)   VALUE 0.
       77 K                 PIC 9(4)   VALUE 0.
       77 P                 PIC 9(4)   VALUE 0.
       77 MAXPOS            PIC 9(4)   VALUE 0.
       77 LEFTLEN           PIC 9(4)   VALUE 0.

       LINKAGE SECTION.

       01 LINKED-ITEMS.
           05 L-A PIC X(100).
           05 L-B PIC X(100).
           05 RESULT PIC 9.

      * You are given 2 words. You need to return True if the second word or any of its rotations is a substring in the first word
      * cycpattern_check("abcd","abd") => False
      * cycpattern_check("hello","ell") => True
      * cycpattern_check("whassup","psus") => False
      * cycpattern_check("abab","baa") => True
      * cycpattern_check("efef","eeff") => False
      * cycpattern_check("himenss","simen") => True
      * 
      * 

      * Complete the WORKING-STORAGE SECTION and the PROCEDURE DIVISION
      * Store the result in the RESULT variable and mark the end of your program with END PROGRAM

       PROCEDURE DIVISION USING LINKED-ITEMS.

       *> Determine trimmed lengths of L-A and L-B (ignore trailing spaces)
           MOVE 100 TO LEN-A
           PERFORM UNTIL LEN-A = 0 OR L-A(LEN-A:1) NOT = SPACE
               SUBTRACT 1 FROM LEN-A
           END-PERFORM

           MOVE 100 TO LEN-B
           PERFORM UNTIL LEN-B = 0 OR L-B(LEN-B:1) NOT = SPACE
               SUBTRACT 1 FROM LEN-B
           END-PERFORM

       *> Copy trimmed strings into working areas
           IF LEN-A > 0
               MOVE L-A(1:LEN-A) TO WA
           ELSE
               MOVE SPACES TO WA
           END-IF

           IF LEN-B > 0
               MOVE L-B(1:LEN-B) TO WB
           ELSE
               MOVE SPACES TO WB
           END-IF

       *> Initialize result to False (0)
           MOVE 0 TO RESULT

       *> Handle edge cases
           IF LEN-B = 0
               MOVE 1 TO RESULT
               GOBACK
           END-IF

           IF LEN-A = 0 OR LEN-B > LEN-A
               MOVE 0 TO RESULT
               GOBACK
           END-IF

       *> Check if any rotation of WB is a substring of WA
           PERFORM VARYING K FROM 0 BY 1
                   UNTIL K > LEN-B - 1 OR RESULT = 1
               COMPUTE LEFTLEN = LEN-B - K
               MOVE SPACES TO ROT
               IF LEFTLEN > 0
                   MOVE WB(K + 1: LEFTLEN) TO ROT(1: LEFTLEN)
               END-IF
               IF K > 0
                   MOVE WB(1: K) TO ROT(LEFTLEN + 1: K)
               END-IF

               COMPUTE MAXPOS = LEN-A - LEN-B + 1
               PERFORM VARYING P FROM 1 BY 1
                       UNTIL P > MAXPOS OR RESULT = 1
                   IF WA(P: LEN-B) = ROT(1: LEN-B)
                       MOVE 1 TO RESULT
                   END-IF
               END-PERFORM
           END-PERFORM

           GOBACK.
       END PROGRAM CYCPATTERN-CHECK.

COBOL_canonical_solution:
IDENTIFICATION DIVISION.
       PROGRAM-ID. EVEN-ODD-COUNT.

       ENVIRONMENT DIVISION.
       
       INPUT-OUTPUT SECTION.

       DATA DIVISION.

       WORKING-STORAGE SECTION.
       
       01 WS-VARIABLES.
           05 WS-NUM PIC S9(10).
           05 WS-DIGIT PIC 9.
           05 WS-EVEN-COUNT PIC S9(10) VALUE 0.
           05 WS-ODD-COUNT PIC S9(10) VALUE 0.
           05 WS-TEMP PIC S9(10).

       LINKAGE SECTION.

       01 LINKED-ITEMS.
           05 L-NUM PIC S9(10).
           05 RESULT OCCURS 100 TIMES INDEXED BY NI PIC S9(10).

      *>Given an integer. return a list that has the number of even and odd digits respectively.
      *>
      *>Example:
      *>even_odd_count(-12) ==> [1, 1]
      *>even_odd_count(123) ==> [1, 2]
      *>

      *>Complete the WORKING-STORAGE SECTION and the PROCEDURE DIVISION
      *>Store the result in the RESULT variable and mark the end of your program with END PROGRAM

       PROCEDURE DIVISION USING LINKED-ITEMS.
       
       MAIN-LOGIC.
           MOVE L-NUM TO WS-NUM
           
      *>   Handle negative numbers by making them positive
           IF WS-NUM < 0
               COMPUTE WS-NUM = WS-NUM * -1
           END-IF
           
      *>   Handle special case of 0
           IF WS-NUM = 0
               MOVE 1 TO WS-EVEN-COUNT
               MOVE 0 TO WS-ODD-COUNT
           ELSE
               PERFORM PROCESS-DIGITS UNTIL WS-NUM = 0
           END-IF
           
      *>   Store results in the RESULT array
           MOVE WS-EVEN-COUNT TO RESULT(1)
           MOVE WS-ODD-COUNT TO RESULT(2)
           
           GOBACK.
           
       PROCESS-DIGITS.
           DIVIDE WS-NUM BY 10 GIVING WS-TEMP REMAINDER WS-DIGIT
           
           IF FUNCTION MOD(WS-DIGIT, 2) = 0
               ADD 1 TO WS-EVEN-COUNT
           ELSE
               ADD 1 TO WS-ODD-COUNT
           END-IF
           
           MOVE WS-TEMP TO WS-NUM.

IDENTIFICATION DIVISION.
       PROGRAM-ID. INT-TO-MINI-ROMAN.

       ENVIRONMENT DIVISION.
       
       INPUT-OUTPUT SECTION.

       DATA DIVISION.

       WORKING-STORAGE SECTION.
       
       
       01 WS-WORK-AREAS.
           05 WS-TEMP-NUM PIC S9(10).
           05 WS-RESULT-PTR PIC 9(3) VALUE 1.
           05 WS-I PIC 9(2).
           
       01 WS-ROMAN-VALUES.
           05 WS-VAL-1000 PIC 9(4) VALUE 1000.
           05 WS-VAL-900 PIC 9(4) VALUE 900.
           05 WS-VAL-500 PIC 9(4) VALUE 500.
           05 WS-VAL-400 PIC 9(4) VALUE 400.
           05 WS-VAL-100 PIC 9(4) VALUE 100.
           05 WS-VAL-90 PIC 9(4) VALUE 90.
           05 WS-VAL-50 PIC 9(4) VALUE 50.
           05 WS-VAL-40 PIC 9(4) VALUE 40.
           05 WS-VAL-10 PIC 9(4) VALUE 10.
           05 WS-VAL-9 PIC 9(4) VALUE 9.
           05 WS-VAL-5 PIC 9(4) VALUE 5.
           05 WS-VAL-4 PIC 9(4) VALUE 4.
           05 WS-VAL-1 PIC 9(4) VALUE 1.
           
       01 WS-ROMAN-NUMERALS.
           05 WS-ROM-1000 PIC X(2) VALUE 'm'.
           05 WS-ROM-900 PIC X(2) VALUE 'cm'.
           05 WS-ROM-500 PIC X(1) VALUE 'd'.
           05 WS-ROM-400 PIC X(2) VALUE 'cd'.
           05 WS-ROM-100 PIC X(1) VALUE 'c'.
           05 WS-ROM-90 PIC X(2) VALUE 'xc'.
           05 WS-ROM-50 PIC X(1) VALUE 'l'.
           05 WS-ROM-40 PIC X(2) VALUE 'xl'.
           05 WS-ROM-10 PIC X(1) VALUE 'x'.
           05 WS-ROM-9 PIC X(2) VALUE 'ix'.
           05 WS-ROM-5 PIC X(1) VALUE 'v'.
           05 WS-ROM-4 PIC X(2) VALUE 'iv'.
           05 WS-ROM-1 PIC X(1) VALUE 'i'.

       LINKAGE SECTION.

       01 LINKED-ITEMS.
           05 L-NUMBER PIC S9(10).
           05 RESULT PIC X(100).

      *>
      *>Given a positive integer, obtain its roman numeral equivalent as a string,
      *>and return it in lowercase.
      *>Restrictions: 1 <= num <= 1000
      *>
      *>Examples:
      *>>>> int_to_mini_roman(19) == 'xix'
      *>>>> int_to_mini_roman(152) == 'clii'
      *>>>> int_to_mini_roman(426) == 'cdxxvi'
      *>

      *>Complete the WORKING-STORAGE SECTION and the PROCEDURE DIVISION
      *>Store the result in the RESULT variable and mark the end of your program with END PROGRAM

       PROCEDURE DIVISION USING LINKED-ITEMS.
       
       MAIN-LOGIC.
           INITIALIZE RESULT
           MOVE 1 TO WS-RESULT-PTR
           MOVE L-NUMBER TO WS-TEMP-NUM
           
           PERFORM PROCESS-1000
           PERFORM PROCESS-900
           PERFORM PROCESS-500
           PERFORM PROCESS-400
           PERFORM PROCESS-100
           PERFORM PROCESS-90
           PERFORM PROCESS-50
           PERFORM PROCESS-40
           PERFORM PROCESS-10
           PERFORM PROCESS-9
           PERFORM PROCESS-5
           PERFORM PROCESS-4
           PERFORM PROCESS-1
           
           GOBACK.
           
       PROCESS-1000.
           PERFORM UNTIL WS-TEMP-NUM < WS-VAL-1000
               STRING WS-ROM-1000 DELIMITED BY SPACE
                      INTO RESULT WITH POINTER WS-RESULT-PTR
               SUBTRACT WS-VAL-1000 FROM WS-TEMP-NUM
           END-PERFORM.
           
       PROCESS-900.
           PERFORM UNTIL WS-TEMP-NUM < WS-VAL-900

IDENTIFICATION DIVISION.
       PROGRAM-ID. INT-TO-MINI-ROMAN.

       ENVIRONMENT DIVISION.
       
       INPUT-OUTPUT SECTION.

       DATA DIVISION.

       LINKAGE SECTION.

       01 LINKED-ITEMS.
           05 L-NUMBER PIC S9(10).
           05 RESULT PIC X(100).

      * 
      * Given a positive integer, obtain its roman numeral equivalent as a string,
      * and return it in lowercase.
      * Restrictions: 1 <= num <= 1000
      * 
      * Examples:
      * >>> int_to_mini_roman(19) == 'xix'
      * >>> int_to_mini_roman(152) == 'clii'
      * >>> int_to_mini_roman(426) == 'cdxxvi'
      * 

      * Complete the WORKING-STORAGE SECTION and the PROCEDURE DIVISION
      * Store the result in the RESULT variable and mark the end of your program with END PROGRAM

       WORKING-STORAGE SECTION.

COBOL_canonical_solution:
IDENTIFICATION DIVISION.
       PROGRAM-ID. RIGHT-ANGLE-TRIANGLE.

       ENVIRONMENT DIVISION.
       
       INPUT-OUTPUT SECTION.

       DATA DIVISION.

       WORKING-STORAGE SECTION.
       
       01 WS-A-SQUARED PIC S9(20).
       01 WS-B-SQUARED PIC S9(20).
       01 WS-C-SQUARED PIC S9(20).
       01 WS-TEMP PIC S9(20).

       LINKAGE SECTION.

       01 LINKED-ITEMS.
           05 L-A PIC S9(10).
           05 L-B PIC S9(10).
           05 L-C PIC S9(10).
           05 RESULT PIC 9.

      *>'''
      *>Given the lengths of the three sides of a triangle. Return True if the three
      *>sides form a right-angled triangle, False otherwise.
      *>A right-angled triangle is a triangle in which one angle is right angle or
      *>90 degree.
      *>Example:
      *>right_angle_triangle(3, 4, 5) == True
      *>right_angle_triangle(1, 2, 3) == False
      *>'''

      *>Complete the WORKING-STORAGE SECTION and the PROCEDURE DIVISION
      *>Store the result in the RESULT variable and mark the end of your program with END PROGRAM

       PROCEDURE DIVISION USING LINKED-ITEMS.
       
       MAIN-LOGIC.
           COMPUTE WS-A-SQUARED = L-A * L-A
           COMPUTE WS-B-SQUARED = L-B * L-B
           COMPUTE WS-C-SQUARED = L-C * L-C
           
           IF WS-A-SQUARED + WS-B-SQUARED = WS-C-SQUARED
               MOVE 1 TO RESULT
           ELSE
               IF WS-A-SQUARED + WS-C-SQUARED = WS-B-SQUARED
                   MOVE 1 TO RESULT
               ELSE
                   IF WS-B-SQUARED + WS-C-SQUARED = WS-A-SQUARED
                       MOVE 1 TO RESULT
                   ELSE
                       MOVE 0 TO RESULT
                   END-IF
               END-IF
           END-IF.
           
           GOBACK.

COBOL_canonical_solution:
IDENTIFICATION DIVISION.
       PROGRAM-ID. FIND-MAX.

       ENVIRONMENT DIVISION.
       DATA DIVISION.
       
       WORKING-STORAGE SECTION.
       01 WS-I            PIC 9.
       01 WS-J            PIC 9.
       01 WS-K            PIC 9.
       01 WS-CHAR         PIC X.
       01 WS-USED-CHARS   PIC X(256).
       01 WS-COUNT        PIC 9(3).
       01 WS-LEN          PIC 9(3).
       01 WS-BEST-COUNT   PIC 9(3) VALUE 0.
       01 WS-BEST-WORD    PIC X(50).
       01 WS-FLAG        PIC 9(4) VALUE 0.
       01 WS-TMP-WORD     PIC X(50).


       LINKAGE SECTION.
       01 LINKED-ITEMS.
           05 L-WORDS OCCURS 3 TIMES INDEXED BY NI PIC X(50).
           05 RESULT PIC X(100).

       

       PROCEDURE DIVISION USING LINKED-ITEMS.

       MAIN-LOGIC.

           MOVE SPACES TO WS-BEST-WORD
           MOVE 0 TO WS-BEST-COUNT

           PERFORM VARYING WS-I FROM 1 BY 1 UNTIL WS-I > 3

               MOVE L-WORDS(WS-I) TO WS-TMP-WORD
               MOVE FUNCTION LENGTH(FUNCTION TRIM(WS-TMP-WORD))
                   TO WS-LEN

               MOVE 0 TO WS-COUNT
               MOVE ALL SPACES TO WS-USED-CHARS

               PERFORM VARYING WS-J FROM 1 BY 1 UNTIL WS-J > WS-LEN
                   MOVE WS-TMP-WORD(WS-J:1) TO WS-CHAR

                   MOVE 0 TO WS-FLAG
                   INSPECT WS-USED-CHARS TALLYING WS-FLAG FOR ALL WS-CHAR

                   IF WS-FLAG = 0
                       ADD 1 TO WS-COUNT
                       MOVE WS-CHAR TO WS-USED-CHARS(WS-COUNT:1)
                   END-IF

               END-PERFORM

               IF WS-COUNT > WS-BEST-COUNT
                   MOVE WS-COUNT TO WS-BEST-COUNT
                   MOVE WS-TMP-WORD TO WS-BEST-WORD
               ELSE
                   IF WS-COUNT = WS-BEST-COUNT
                       IF WS-TMP-WORD < WS-BEST-WORD
                           MOVE WS-TMP-WORD TO WS-BEST-WORD
                       END-IF
                   END-IF
               END-IF

           END-PERFORM

           MOVE WS-BEST-WORD TO RESULT

           EXIT PROGRAM.
       END PROGRAM FIND-MAX.

COBOL_canonical_solution:
IDENTIFICATION DIVISION.
       PROGRAM-ID. EAT.

       ENVIRONMENT DIVISION.
       
       INPUT-OUTPUT SECTION.

       DATA DIVISION.

       WORKING-STORAGE SECTION.
       
       01 WS-EATEN PIC S9(10).
       01 WS-LEFT PIC S9(10).
       01 WS-CAN-EAT PIC S9(10).

       LINKAGE SECTION.

       01 LINKED-ITEMS.
           05 L-NUMBER PIC S9(10).
           05 L-NEED PIC S9(10).
           05 L-REMAINING PIC S9(10).
           05 RESULT OCCURS 100 TIMES INDEXED BY NI PIC S9(10).

      *>
      *>You're a hungry rabbit, and you already have eaten a certain number of carrots,
      *>but now you need to eat more carrots to complete the day's meals.
      *>you should return an array of [ total number of eaten carrots after your meals,
      *>the number of carrots left after your meals ]
      *>if there are not enough remaining carrots, you will eat all remaining carrots, but will still be hungry.
      *>
      *>Example:
      *>* eat(5, 6, 10) -> [11, 4]
      *>* eat(4, 8, 9) -> [12, 1]
      *>* eat(1, 10, 10) -> [11, 0]
      *>* eat(2, 11, 5) -> [7, 0]
      *>
      *>Variables:
      *>@number : integer
      *>the number of carrots that you have eaten.
      *>@need : integer
      *>the number of carrots that you need to eat.
      *>@remaining : integer
      *>the number of remaining carrots thet exist in stock
      *>
      *>Constrain:
      *>* 0 <= number <= 1000
      *>* 0 <= need <= 1000
      *>* 0 <= remaining <= 1000
      *>
      *>Have fun :)
      *>

      *>Complete the WORKING-STORAGE SECTION and the PROCEDURE DIVISION
      *>Store the result in the RESULT variable and mark the end of your program with END PROGRAM

       PROCEDURE DIVISION USING LINKED-ITEMS.

      *>Calculate how many carrots can actually be eaten
      *>(minimum of what's needed and what's remaining)
           IF L-NEED <= L-REMAINING
               MOVE L-NEED TO WS-CAN-EAT
           ELSE
               MOVE L-REMAINING TO WS-CAN-EAT
           END-IF.

      *>Calculate total eaten carrots
           COMPUTE WS-EATEN = L-NUMBER + WS-CAN-EAT.

      *>Calculate remaining carrots after eating
           COMPUTE WS-LEFT = L-REMAINING - WS-CAN-EAT.

      *>Store results in the RESULT array
           MOVE WS-EATEN TO RESULT(1).
           MOVE WS-LEFT TO RESULT(2).

           GOBACK.

import java.util.*;
import java.lang.*;

class Solution {
    /**
    You're a hungry rabbit, and you already have eaten a certain number of carrots,
    but now you need to eat more carrots to complete the day's meals.
    you should return an array of [ total number of eaten carrots after your meals,
                                    the number of carrots left after your meals ]
    if there are not enough remaining carrots, you will eat all remaining carrots, but will still be hungry.

IDENTIFICATION DIVISION.
       PROGRAM-ID. DO-ALGEBRA.

       ENVIRONMENT DIVISION.
       DATA DIVISION.

       WORKING-STORAGE SECTION.

       01 MAX-SIZE        PIC 9(3) VALUE 10.
       01 WS-I            PIC 9(3).
       01 WS-N            PIC 9(3).
       01 WS-K            PIC 9(3).

       01 WS-A            PIC S9(10).
       01 WS-B            PIC S9(10).
       01 WS-RES          PIC S9(10).
       01 WS-EXP-RES      PIC S9(10).
       01 WS-DIV-RES      PIC S9(10).
       01 WS-DIV-REM      PIC S9(10).

        LINKAGE SECTION.
       01 LINKED-ITEMS.
           05 L-OPERATOR OCCURS 3 TIMES INDEXED BY NI PIC X(3).
           05 L-OPERAND OCCURS 4 TIMES INDEXED BY NJ PIC S9(10).
           05 RESULT PIC S9(10).

       PROCEDURE DIVISION USING LINKED-ITEMS.

       MAIN-LOGIC.

           
           MOVE 3 to WS-N

           PERFORM VARYING WS-I FROM 1 BY 1 UNTIL WS-I > WS-N
               IF L-OPERATOR(WS-I) = "**"
                   MOVE L-OPERAND(WS-I) TO WS-A
                   MOVE L-OPERAND(WS-I + 1) TO WS-B
                   MOVE 1 TO WS-EXP-RES
                   PERFORM VARYING WS-K FROM 1 BY 1 UNTIL WS-K > WS-B 
                       MULTIPLY WS-EXP-RES BY WS-A GIVING WS-EXP-RES
                       
                   END-PERFORM
                   MOVE WS-EXP-RES TO L-OPERAND(WS-I)

                   PERFORM VARYING WS-K FROM WS-I BY 1 UNTIL WS-K > WS-N
                       MOVE L-OPERATOR(WS-K + 1) TO L-OPERATOR(WS-K)
                       MOVE L-OPERAND(WS-K + 2) TO L-OPERAND(WS-K + 1)
                   END-PERFORM
                   SUBTRACT 1 FROM WS-N
                   SUBTRACT 1 FROM WS-I
               END-IF
           END-PERFORM

           PERFORM VARYING WS-I FROM 1 BY 1 UNTIL WS-I > WS-N
               IF L-OPERATOR(WS-I) = "*" OR L-OPERATOR(WS-I) = "//"
                   MOVE L-OPERAND(WS-I) TO WS-A
                   MOVE L-OPERAND(WS-I + 1) TO WS-B

                   IF L-OPERATOR(WS-I) = "*"
                       MULTIPLY WS-A BY WS-B
                       MOVE WS-B TO L-OPERAND(WS-I)
                   ELSE
                       DIVIDE WS-A BY WS-B
                           GIVING WS-DIV-RES
                           REMAINDER WS-DIV-REM
                       MOVE WS-DIV-RES TO L-OPERAND(WS-I)
                   END-IF
                   
                    

                   PERFORM VARYING WS-K FROM WS-I BY 1 UNTIL WS-K > WS-N
                       MOVE L-OPERATOR(WS-K + 1) TO L-OPERATOR(WS-K)
                       MOVE L-OPERAND(WS-K + 2) TO L-OPERAND(WS-K + 1)
                   END-PERFORM
                   SUBTRACT 1 FROM WS-N
                   SUBTRACt 1 FROM WS-I
               END-IF
           END-PERFORM
           
           MOVE L-OPERAND(1) TO RESULT
           PERFORM VARYING WS-I FROM 1 BY 1 UNTIL WS-I > WS-N
               MOVE L-OPERAND(WS-I + 1) TO WS-B
               IF L-OPERATOR(WS-I) = "+"
                   ADD WS-B TO RESULT
               ELSE
                   SUBTRACT WS-B FROM RESULT
               END-IF
           END-PERFORM

       STOP RUN.

IDENTIFICATION DIVISION.
       PROGRAM-ID. SOLVE.

       ENVIRONMENT DIVISION.
       
       INPUT-OUTPUT SECTION.

       DATA DIVISION.

       WORKING-STORAGE SECTION.
       
       
       01 WS-FROM-SET PIC X(52) VALUE "abcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyzABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXYZ".
       01 WS-TO-SET   PIC X(52) VALUE "ABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXYZabcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz".
       01 WS-LEN      PIC 9(3) VALUE 0.
       01 I           PIC 9(3) VALUE 0.
       01 J           PIC 9(3) VALUE 0.
       01 HAS-LETTER  PIC 9 VALUE 0.

       LINKAGE SECTION.

       01 LINKED-ITEMS.
           05 L-S PIC X(100).
           05 RESULT PIC X(100).

      * You are given a string s.
      * if s[i] is a letter, reverse its case from lower to upper or vise versa,
      * otherwise keep it as it is.
      * If the string contains no letters, reverse the string.
      * The function should return the resulted string.
      * Examples
      * solve("1234") = "4321"
      * solve("ab") = "AB"
      * solve("#a@C") = "#A@c"
      * 

      * Complete the WORKING-STORAGE SECTION and the PROCEDURE DIVISION
      * Store the result in the RESULT variable and mark the end of your program with END PROGRAM

       PROCEDURE DIVISION USING LINKED-ITEMS.
           MOVE SPACES TO RESULT.

           MOVE 100 TO I
           PERFORM UNTIL I = 0 OR L-S(I:1) NOT = SPACE
               SUBTRACT 1 FROM I
           END-PERFORM
           MOVE I TO WS-LEN

           MOVE 0 TO HAS-LETTER
           IF WS-LEN > 0
               PERFORM VARYING I FROM 1 BY 1 UNTIL I > WS-LEN OR HAS-LETTER = 1
                   IF L-S(I:1) IS ALPHABETIC
                       MOVE 1 TO HAS-LETTER
                   END-IF
               END-PERFORM
           END-IF

           IF HAS-LETTER = 1
               MOVE L-S(1:WS-LEN) TO RESULT(1:WS-LEN)
               INSPECT RESULT(1:WS-LEN)
                   CONVERTING WS-FROM-SET TO WS-TO-SET
           ELSE
               IF WS-LEN > 0
                   PERFORM VARYING I FROM 1 BY 1 UNTIL I > WS-LEN
                       COMPUTE J = WS-LEN - I + 1
                       MOVE L-S(J:1) TO RESULT(I:1)
                   END-PERFORM
               END-IF
           END-IF

           GOBACK.
       END PROGRAM SOLVE.

COBOL_canonical_solution:
IDENTIFICATION DIVISION.
       PROGRAM-ID. GENERATE-INTEGERS.

       ENVIRONMENT DIVISION.
       
       INPUT-OUTPUT SECTION.

       DATA DIVISION.

       WORKING-STORAGE SECTION.
       
       01 WS-VARIABLES.
           05 WS-START PIC S9(10).
           05 WS-END PIC S9(10).
           05 WS-CURRENT PIC S9(10).
           05 WS-INDEX PIC S9(3) VALUE 1.
           05 WS-TEMP PIC S9(10).

       LINKAGE SECTION.

       01 LINKED-ITEMS.
           05 L-A PIC S9(10).
           05 L-B PIC S9(10).
           05 RESULT OCCURS 100 TIMES INDEXED BY NI PIC S9(10).

      *>
      *>Given two positive integers a and b, return the even digits between a
      *>and b, in ascending order.
      *>
      *>For example:
      *>generate_integers(2, 8) => [2, 4, 6, 8]
      *>generate_integers(8, 2) => [2, 4, 6, 8]
      *>generate_integers(10, 14) => []
      *>

      *>Complete the WORKING-STORAGE SECTION and the PROCEDURE DIVISION
      *>Store the result in the RESULT variable and mark the end of your program with END PROGRAM

       PROCEDURE DIVISION USING LINKED-ITEMS.

       MAIN-LOGIC.
      *>Initialize result array
           PERFORM VARYING NI FROM 1 BY 1 UNTIL NI > 100
               MOVE ZERO TO RESULT(NI)
           END-PERFORM
           
      *>Determine start and end values (handle both a<=b and a>b cases)
           IF L-A <= L-B
               MOVE L-A TO WS-START
               MOVE L-B TO WS-END
           ELSE
               MOVE L-B TO WS-START
               MOVE L-A TO WS-END
           END-IF
           
      *>Initialize index for result array
           MOVE 1 TO WS-INDEX
           
      *>Generate even digits between start and end
           PERFORM VARYING WS-CURRENT FROM WS-START BY 1 
               UNTIL WS-CURRENT > WS-END OR WS-INDEX > 100
               
      *>Check if current number is a single even digit (0-9)
               IF WS-CURRENT >= 0 AND WS-CURRENT <= 9
                   DIVIDE WS-CURRENT BY 2 GIVING WS-TEMP REMAINDER WS-TEMP
                   IF WS-TEMP = 0
                       MOVE WS-CURRENT TO RESULT(WS-INDEX)
                       ADD 1 TO WS-INDEX
                   END-IF
               END-IF
           END-PERFORM

           GOBACK.
